rationale: Both correctly diagnose the #4527 regression and confirm the #4792 fix already lives in the 0.24 rebase commit cited by the maintainers, matching the thread's actual resolution. X grounds slightly better with more file:line citations (scheduling coordinator's _FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES, timeout log sources) and explicitly triages red herrings in the reporter's environment (NaN-clamp patch, min_tokens/#4962), giving a more complete picture; it also offers three concrete remediation paths (upgrade/cherry-pick/workaround). Y is comparably strong but cites a suspiciously specific unit test name (`test_build_omni_output_non_async_chunk_accumulates_full_payload`) with no support in the provided ground truth, which reads as a plausible-sounding fabrication and slightly dings its grounding.
